segments.text: After this I saw another angel descending from heaven with great authority, and the earth was illuminated by his glory.
And he cried out in a mighty voice:

Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! [^44] She has become a lair for demons and a haunt for every unclean spirit, every unclean bird, 
and every detestable beast. [^45]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
All the nations have drunk[^46] the wineof the passion of her immorality. The kings of the earth were immoral with her, and the merchants of the earth have grown wealthy
from the extravagance of her luxury.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then I heard another voice from heaven say:

Come out of her, My people, [^47] so that you will not share in her sins
or contract any of her plagues.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For her sins are piled up to heaven, and God has remembered her iniquities.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Give back to her as she has done to others; pay her back double for what she has done; 
mix her a double portion in her own cup.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As much as she has glorified herself and lived in luxury, give her the same measure of torment and grief. In her heart she says, ‘I sit as queen; I am not a widow and will never see grief.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore her plagues will come in one day—death and grief and famine—and she will be consumed by fire, for mighty is the Lord God who judges her.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the kings of the earth who committed sexual immorality and lived in luxury with her will weep and wail at the sight of the smoke rising from the fire that consumes her. [^48]
In fear of her torment, they will st and at a distance and cry out:
Woe, woe to the great city, the mighty city of Babylon! For in a single houryour judgment has come.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And the merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, because there is no one left to buy their cargo—
cargo of gold, silver, precious stones, and pearls; of fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; of all kinds of citron wood and every article of ivory, precious wood, bronze, iron, and marble;
of cinnamon, spice, incense, myrrh, and frankincense; of wine, olive oil, fine flour, and wheat; of cattle, sheep, horses, and chariots; of slaves and souls of men.
And they will say:[^49]
The fruit of your soul’s desirehas departed from you; all your luxury and splendor have vanished, never to be seen again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The merchants who sold these things and grew their wealth from her will st and at a distance, in fear of her torment. They will weep and mourn,
saying:

Woe, woe to the great city, clothed in fine linen and purple and scarlet, 
adorned with gold and precious stones and pearls!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For in a single hoursuch fabulous wealth has been destroyed!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Every shipmaster, passenger, and sailor, and all who make their living from the sea, will st and at a distance
and cry out at the sight of the smoke rising from the fire that consumes her.
What city was ever like this great city?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they will exclaim.
Then they will throw dust on their heads as they weep and mourn and cry out:

Woe, woe to the great city, where all who had ships on the sea
were enriched by her wealth! For in a single hourshe has been destroyed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Rejoice over her, O heaven, O saints and apostles and prophets, because God has pronounced for youHis judgment against her.
Then a mighty angel picked up a stone the size of a great millstone and cast it into the sea, saying:

With such violencethe great city of Babylon will be cast down, 
never to be seen again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
And the sound of harpists and musicians, of flute players and trumpeters, 
will never ring out in you again. Nor will any craftsmen of any tradebe found in you again, nor the sound of a millstonebe heard in you again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The light of a lampwill never shine in you again, and the voices of a bride and bridegroomwill never call out in you again. For your merchants were the great ones of the earth, because all the nations were deceived by your sorcery.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And there was found in her the blood of prophets and saints, and of all who had been slain on the earth.
